name: 广州
description: Guangzhou, the capital of Guangdong province, is a historic trading port and the birthplace of Cantonese culture. Known as 'Canton', it offers world-renowned dim sum, fascinating food markets, and the iconic Canton Tower. The city blends Southern Chinese traditions with modern architecture, and serves as the gateway to Hong Kong and Macau.
highlights: Chen Clan Ancestral Hall - Traditional Cantonese architecture
Beijing Rd Pedestrian Street - Historic shopping district
Baiyun Mountain - City views and tea houses

climate.tips: Best visited in autumn and early spring. Summers are hot, humid, and rainy (May-September). Typhoon season is July-September. Light clothing most of the year. Mosquitoes common in summer.

attractions.name: 上下九步行街
attractions.description: Guangzhou's most famous pedestrian shopping street with traditional Cantonese architecture and local snacks.
attractions.tips: Best in evening for neon lights. Try the beef offal and rice rolls from street vendors. Bargaining expected at traditional shops.

attractions.name: 北京路步行街
attractions.description: One of Guangzhou's oldest commercial districts with ancient ruins visible through glass floors.
attractions.tips: Visit the ancient ruins museum under the glass floor. Combine with nearby Yuexiu Park. Good for evening strolls.

attractions.name: 广东省博物馆
attractions.description: Modern museum with exhibitions on Guangdong's history, culture, and natural resources.
attractions.tips: Book tickets via official WeChat account. Combine with Guangzhou Library and Opera House nearby.

attractions.name: 广州动物园
attractions.description: Large zoological park with diverse animals and conservation programs.
attractions.tips: Arrive early for panda viewing. Bring water and sunscreen. Weekday visits less crowded.

attractions.name: 百万葵园
attractions.description: Theme park with millions of sunflowers and various flower displays throughout the year.
attractions.tips: Best during flower season (March-November). Located far from city center. Combine with Nansha wet market.

attractions.name: 从化温泉
attractions.description: Famous hot spring resort area with mineral-rich geothermal springs.
attractions.tips: Book accommodation with private pools for best experience. Bring swimwear. Best as day trip or overnight.

attractions.name: 南沙湿地公园
attractions.description: Coastal wetland with mangrove forests, bird watching, and scenic boat tours.
attractions.tips: Best in autumn and winter for migratory birds. Take the boat tour for the full experience. Bring binoculars.

attractions.name: 余荫山房
attractions.description: A classic Lingnan garden showcasing traditional Guangdong residential architecture.
attractions.tips: One of the four famous Lingnan gardens. Best with guide to explain the symbolism. Combine with nearby Lotus Mountain.

attractions.name: 岭南印象园
attractions.description: Cultural theme park recreating traditional Lingnan architecture, food, and crafts.
attractions.tips: Evening performances are highlight. Try traditional snacks like rice noodles and egg tarts. Great for cultural photos.

attractions.name: 宝墨园
attractions.description: A large traditional Chinese garden with ancient architecture and goldfish pond.
attractions.tips: Less crowded than other attractions. Combine with nearby Guangzhou Ouroi Street. Great for traditional Chinese photography.

attractions.name: 白水寨风景区
attractions.description: Mountain area with the highest waterfall in Guangdong and scenic hiking trails.
attractions.tips: Start hiking early morning. Bring hiking shoes. The air is fresh and负离子含量高. Summer best for waterfalls.

attractions.name: 大夫山森林公园
attractions.description: Large urban forest park with cycling paths, lakes, and picnic areas.
attractions.tips: Rent bikes at entrance. Best in autumn and spring. Less crowded on weekdays. Perfect for family outings.

attractions.name: 广州塔
attractions.description: The iconic TV tower with a unique 'fish belly' design, offering panoramic city views and the world's highest Skywalk.
attractions.tips: Book evening tickets for best views and light show. Sunset timing is magical. Lower decks less crowded.

attractions.name: 陈家祠
attractions.description: A meticulously preserved traditional Cantonese building complex with intricate woodcarvings and stone sculptures.
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekdays. Excellent traditional crafts inside. Combined with Shamian Island walk.

attractions.name: 沙面
attractions.description: A former foreign concession with beautiful European architecture, tree-lined streets, and colonial-era buildings.
attractions.tips: Best in afternoon for photos. Combine with nearby Huangshatang food street. Great for wedding photos.

attractions.name: 白云山
attractions.description: A city park with peaks offering respite from the urban bustle and panoramic views of Guangzhou.
attractions.tips: Take the cable car up, walk down for different views. Bring water and snacks. Morning visits cooler.

attractions.name: 珠江夜游
attractions.description: A scenic cruise along the Pearl River showcasing Guangzhou's illuminated skyline and historic bridges.
attractions.tips: Book through hotel concierge. Outdoor decks colder in winter. Best in clear weather.

attractions.name: 六榕寺
attractions.description: A famous Buddhist temple dating from 537 AD with an iconic 9-story pagoda and ancient banyan tree.
attractions.tips: One of Guangzhou's oldest temples. The pagoda offers views over the city. Best in morning for fewer worshippers.

attractions.name: 广州大剧院
attractions.description: Zaha Hadid's masterpiece of curved concrete and glass on the Pearl River, a world-class performing arts venue.
attractions.tips: Best photographed from across the river at night. Book a show for the full experience. Combine with Canton Tower visit.

attractions.name: 东山口
attractions.description: Historic Cantonese neighborhood with European-style villas, art galleries, trendy cafes, and tree-lined streets.
attractions.tips: Best explored on foot. Great for brunch and coffee. Combine with nearby Taojiang Rd for art galleries.

attractions.name: 海珠湿地公园
attractions.description: A beautiful wetland park in the heart of the city with water channels, lotus ponds, and bird-watching opportunities.
attractions.tips: Best in lotus season (June-August). Wear comfortable shoes for walking. Great escape from city bustle.

attractions.name: 黄埔古港
attractions.description: The historic departure point of China's maritime Silk Road, with traditional architecture and Cantonese food stalls.
attractions.tips: Try the famous ginger milk pudding (姜撞奶) here. Combine with nearby Whampoa Military Academy.

attractions.name: 越秀公园
attractions.description: Guangzhou's largest park featuring the ancient Zhenhai Tower, beautiful gardens, and the iconic Five Goat Statue.
attractions.tips: Start here for history and city views. Take the cable car for panoramic views. Best in early morning.

attractions.name: 南沙天后宫
attractions.description: A magnificent Mazu temple by the sea, featuring traditional Chinese palace architecture and ocean views.
attractions.tips: Combine with nearby beaches. Best visited in morning for fewer crowds. Great for photos.

attractions.name: 中山公园
attractions.description: One of China's oldest parks, featuring traditional Chinese gardens, amusement rides, and cultural exhibitions.
attractions.tips: Good for families with children. Weekend visits popular. Combine with Shangxiajiu nearby.

attractions.name: 黄埔军校旧址
attractions.description: The historic military academy where many famous Chinese generals trained, now a museum.
attractions.tips: Take ferry from Huangpu Ancient Harbor. Best with historical context. Audio guides helpful.

attractions.name: 六榕塔
attractions.description: An ancient Buddhist pagoda built in 896 AD, part of the Six Banyan Temple complex.
attractions.tips: Climb to top for panoramic views. Best combined with Six Banyan Temple visit.

attractions.name: 广州图书馆
attractions.description: A modern public library with extensive collections, reading rooms, and cultural events.
attractions.tips: Bring ID for entry. Combine with nearby Guangdong Museum and Opera House.

attractions.name: 荔枝湾涌
attractions.description: A renovated waterway system with traditional Lingnan architecture, boat rides, and street food.
attractions.tips: Best in evening with lights. Try boat ride for unique perspective. Combine with Chen Clan Ancestral Hall.

attractions.name: 广州塔摩天轮
attractions.description: 世界最高的摩天轮之一。
attractions.tips: 夜景最美。

attractions.name: 沙面岛
attractions.description: 珠江中的小岛，有大量欧式建筑。
attractions.tips: 拍照打卡。

attractions.name: 白云山
attractions.description: 广州最著名的山，可俯瞰城市全景。
attractions.tips: 登高望远。

attractions.name: 珠江夜游
attractions.description: 乘船游览珠江两岸夜景。
attractions.tips: 夜游珠江。

attractions.name: 广东省博物馆
attractions.description: 馆藏文物17万件，以潮州木雕最著名。
attractions.tips: 了解广东文化。

attractions.name: 北京路
attractions.description: 广州最古老的商业街，有千年古道遗址。
attractions.tips: 购物好去处。

attractions.name: 光孝寺
attractions.description: 广州最古老的佛教寺院，有1700多年历史。
attractions.tips: 了解佛教文化。

attractions.name: 越秀公园
attractions.description: 广州最大的公园，有五羊雕像。
attractions.tips: 广州标志。

attractions.name: 长隆旅游度假区
attractions.description: 中国最大的主题乐园之一。
attractions.tips: 适合亲子游。

attractions.name: 广州美食
attractions.description: 广州是中国美食之都，有早茶和各种粤菜。
attractions.tips: 品尝广州美食。

attractions.name: 天河商圈
attractions.description: 广州最繁华的商业区。
attractions.tips: 购物好去处。

attractions.name: 广州博物馆
attractions.description: 展示广州历史的博物馆。
attractions.tips: 了解广州文化。

attractions.name: 黄埔古港
attractions.description: 广州古代海上丝绸之路的起点。
attractions.tips: 了解广州贸易史。

attractions.name: 广州美术馆
attractions.description: 展示中外美术作品。
attractions.tips: 了解艺术文化。

attractions.name: 广州夜景
attractions.description: 珠江新城和广州塔的夜景灯光秀。
attractions.tips: 夏季夜间最美。

attractions.name: 荔湾区
attractions.description: 广州老城区，有西关大屋和各种小吃。
attractions.tips: 了解老广州。

attractions.name: 流花湖公园
attractions.description: 广州最美的公园之一，有湖泊和花园。
attractions.tips: 休闲好去处。

attractions.name: 陈家祠
attractions.description: 广东最大的祠堂建筑群，有精美的木雕和砖雕。
attractions.tips: 了解岭南文化。

attractions.name: 广州地铁
attractions.description: 中国第三大城市轨道交通系统。
attractions.tips: 体验广州交通。

attractions.name: 广州白云国际机场
attractions.description: 中国三大枢纽机场之一。
attractions.tips: 了解广州交通。

attractions.name: 广州大桥
attractions.description: 横跨珠江的大桥。
attractions.tips: 看珠江风光。

restaurants.name: 莲记糖水铺

restaurants.name: 陈添记鱼皮

restaurants.name: 榕记港式茶餐厅

restaurants.name: 点心皇子

restaurants.name: 潮汕鱼粥

restaurants.name: 粤菜王

arrival.tips: Second busiest airport in China. Metro Line 3 connects to city center. High-speed rail station nearby.

arrival.to: Guangzhou South Railway Station / Guangzhou Railway Station
arrival.tips: High-speed rail very convenient. Guangzhou South is for high-speed, Guangzhou for conventional trains.

metro: Line 3 very crowded during peak hours
bus: Extensive network but complex to navigate
Buses marked with numbers and pinyin
taxi: Didi app fully English-friendly in Guangzhou

hotels.bookingTips: Riverside rooms worth the extra cost.

hotels.name: 广州白天鹅宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: Request rooms with river views. Famous for breakfast dim sum.

hotels.name: 广州万豪酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best river-view rooms on higher floors.

hotels.name: 广州太古汇 Hyatt Regency

hotels.bookingTips: Request rooms away from street for quiet.

hotels.name: 广州文华东方酒店

hotels.name: 广州富力丽思卡尔顿酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Club level rooms include excellent breakfast.

hotels.name: 广州粤海威斯汀酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Heavenly beds popular for good sleep.

hotels.name: 广州白云机场铂尔曼酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Perfect for early flights or late arrivals.

hotels.name: 广州亚朵酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good value for quality conscious travelers.

hotels.name: 广州全季酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good balance of price and quality.

payment.description: Dominant payment in Guangzhou, accepted everywhere
tips: Guangzhou is modern - foreign cards work at many places
Even locals prefer Alipay for larger payments

payment.description: Still useful for traditional markets and small vendors

culturalTips.title: Cantonese Language Culture
culturalTips.content: Cantonese (广东话/粤语) is the local language with 9 tones, very different from Mandarin. Locals appreciate when visitors try basic phrases like 'nei hou' (hello) and 'm goi' (thank you). Many shop signs are in traditional Chinese characters.

culturalTips.title: Yum Cha (饮茶) Culture
culturalTips.content: Morning tea (饮茶/yum cha) is a sacred social ritual in Guangzhou. Locals spend hours at dim sum restaurants with family and friends. Push carts through the restaurant - grab what looks good. Tip the tea waiter by pouring tea for them first.

culturalTips.title: Cantonese Food Culture
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou is the birthplace of dim sum. Try morning tea (yum cha) culture - it's a social ritual. Fresh and light flavors are prized.

culturalTips.title: Cantonese Dining Etiquette
culturalTips.content: When dining in Guangzhou: never stick chopsticks vertically in rice (funeral tradition), leave some food on plate to show fullness, turn鱼(upside down) when finishing fish dish, tap three fingers to thank server.

culturalTips.title: Local Greetings
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou locals greet each other with '食左未' (have you eaten?) instead of hello. It's a genuine question - feel free to respond with your meal status. This reflects the food-centric culture.

culturalTips.title: Hot Spring Bathing Culture
culturalTips.content: 从化温泉 (Conghua Hot Springs) is a popular weekend retreat. Locals believe hot spring bathing has health benefits. Etiquette: shower before entering, stay hydrated, limit sessions to 15-20 minutes each.

culturalTips.title: Cantonese New Year Traditions
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou has unique¥ traditions: flower markets (年宵花市), eating reunion dinner with abalone and goose, giving red envelopes (利是) with brand new bills, watching fireworks at Licheng.

culturalTips.title: Market Bargaining Culture
culturalTips.content: Bargaining is expected at traditional markets (上下九, Sanlin Road). Start at 30-40% of asking price and meet in middle. Always smile and be respectful - it's a social interaction, not confrontation.

culturalTips.title: Cantonese Tea History
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou was the first Chinese port to trade tea with Britain in 1700s. The phrase 'Canton tea' originally referred to all Chinese tea exports. Today, people here still take tea very seriously with oldest teahouses like 莲香楼 dating to 1889.

culturalTips.title: Lingnan Architecture
culturalTips.content: Lingnan architecture (岭南建筑) blends Chinese and Western influences unique to Guangdong. Look for: bars on windows (趟栊门), decorative screen walls (照壁), and European colonial elements like in 沙面.

culturalTips.title: Cantonese Superstitions
culturalTips.content: Common superstitions: don't buy clocks as gifts (sounds like death), number 4 is unlucky, hang mirrors outside to deflect bad luck, red brings good fortune especially during festivals and New Year.

culturalTips.title: Foot Massage Culture
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou has many foot massage (沐足) shops - it's a legitimate relaxation activity enjoyed by locals of all ages. A typical session costs 50-150¥ for 60-90 minutes. Very popular after work.

culturalTips.title: History of Dim Sum
culturalTips.content: Dim sum (点心) literally means 'touch the heart' - small dishes meant to be shared. It originated in Guangzhou teahouses along the Silk Road. Original items included lotus leaf rice wraps and shrimp dumplings.

culturalTips.title: Cantonese vs Mandarin
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou locals speak Cantonese (广东话) natively, though Mandarin is also understood. English more commonly spoken here than in northern cities.

culturalTips.title: Humid Weather Adaptation
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou is very humid year-round. Stay hydrated, use sun protection, and acclimatize to the warmth.

culturalTips.title: Cantonese Breakfast
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou locals enjoy '早茶' (morning tea) as a social ritual. Arrive at dim sum restaurants around 9-10am. It's normal to spend 2+ hours finishing multiple courses of dumplings, buns, and rice rolls.

culturalTips.title: Seafood Dining Culture
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou's proximity to the sea means fresh seafood is a staple. Live fish and shellfish are common, with '清蒸' (steamed) being the preferred cooking method to preserve natural flavors.

culturalTips.title: Maritime Silk Road Heritage
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou was the starting point of the Maritime Silk Road. The 黄埔古港 (Huangpu Ancient Harbor) is where Chinese merchants first traded with foreigners, shaping the city's unique multicultural heritage.

culturalTips.title: Dragon Boat Festival Traditions
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou takes dragon boat racing seriously. The Tuangong Festival features elaborate races along the Pearl River. Try zongzi (sticky rice dumplings) during this season.

culturalTips.title: Cantonese Fashion Culture
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou is a major fashion manufacturing hub. The 上下九 area has shops selling cheongsam (tail bar) and traditional Cantonese clothing. Shopping here is an experience, not just a transaction.

culturalTips.title: Night Market Culture
culturalTips.content: Night markets (夜市) are popular in Guangzhou. Try street food like stinky tofu, oyster pancakes, and grilled squid. Look for markets near 北京路 and 上下九.
